title: Arrays & Objects
description: Master JavaScript arrays and objects for storing and manipulating complex data structures.
learningObjectives: Apply array/object operations in real scenarios

subtopics.title: Array Basics
subtopics.content:
Arrays store multiple values in a single variable:

**Creating Arrays:**
```javascript
let fruits = ['apple', 'banana', 'orange'];
let numbers = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
let mixed = ['text', 42, true, null];
```

**Accessing Elements:**
```javascript
let colors = ['red', 'green', 'blue'];
console.log(colors[0]); // 'red' (first element)
console.log(colors[2]); // 'blue' (third element)
console.log(colors.length); // 3 (array length)
```

**Array Indexing:**
- Arrays are zero-indexed (starts at 0)
- Last element: array[array.length - 1]
- Negative indices don't work in JavaScript

subtopics.title: Array Methods
subtopics.content:
JavaScript provides powerful array methods:

**Adding/Removing:**
```javascript
let items = ['a', 'b', 'c'];
items.push('d'); // Add to end: ['a', 'b', 'c', 'd']
items.pop(); // Remove from end: ['a', 'b', 'c']
items.unshift('start'); // Add to beginning
items.shift(); // Remove from beginning
```

**Searching:**
```javascript
let numbers = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
console.log(numbers.includes(3)); // true
console.log(numbers.indexOf(4)); // 3
console.log(numbers.find(x => x > 2)); // 3
```

**Transforming:**
```javascript
let nums = [1, 2, 3, 4];
let doubled = nums.map(x => x * 2); // [2, 4, 6, 8]
let evens = nums.filter(x => x % 2 === 0); // [2, 4]
let sum = nums.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0); // 10
```

Object Methods & Arrays of Objects

glossary.definition: An ordered collection of values that can be accessed by index.
A collection of key-value pairs for storing structured data.
A function that is a property of an object.
A key-value pair in an object that stores data.
